data.title: Validate session persistence across Bash invocations
data.description:
## Description
Validate that @playwright/cli maintains browser session state across separate Bash invocations when using the same -s=name session identifier.

Steps:
1. Teardown: close any existing spike-test session (npx @playwright/cli close -s=spike-test 2>/dev/null || true)
2. Navigate to a test page in one Bash call with -s=spike-test
3. In a separate Bash call with same -s=spike-test, click an element and verify page state
4. Verify: the second call sees the state from the first call
5. Cleanup: close the spike-test session after test (npx @playwright/cli close -s=spike-test)

The session teardown/cleanup must be part of the test to prevent zombie Chromium processes.
Note: Tasks 3, 4, 5 must run sequentially (not in parallel) to avoid Chromium session conflicts.

## File Impact
- tests/scripts/test-playwright-cli-validation.sh (EDIT — update session persistence test)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Session persistence test passes
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-playwright-cli-validation.sh 2>&1 | grep "test_session_persistence" | grep -q "PASS"
- [ ] No orphaned Chromium processes after test
  Verify: ! pgrep -f "chromium.*spike-test" >/dev/null 2>&1
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
